axiom R2 : BFumbles → BLose        -- If Team B fumbles a lot, they lose
axiom R3 : AScoreMore ∨ BFumbles   -- Either A scores more or B fumbles (or both)

by
  intro hNotLose
  apply Or.elim R3
  · intro aMore
    exact aMore
  · intro bFumbles
    have bLose : BLose := R2 bFumbles
    exact False.elim (hNotLose bLose)
</lean>

axiom Friday : Prop
axiom GoParty : obj → Prop
axiom Watch : obj → Prop
axiom Finish : obj → Prop
axiom Study : obj → Prop
axiom Plan : obj → Prop

-- Assumptions extracted from the context
axiom student_James : Student James
-- If it is Friday night, then (students) are going to the party
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → (Friday → GoParty x)
-- If they finish all their homework, then they can watch the game
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Finish x → Watch x
-- Either it is Friday night, or (students) are not watching the game
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → (Friday ∨ ¬Watch x)
-- If (students) study everyday, they finish all their homework
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Student x → (Study x → Finish x)
-- If someone plans their schedule well, then they can study everyday
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, Plan x → Study x

-- Given in the statement to evaluate
axiom not_party_James : ¬GoParty James

theorem james_not_planned_well : ¬Plan James := by
  intro hPlan
  have hFriToParty : Friday → GoParty James := R1 James student_James
  have hNotFriday : ¬Friday := by
    intro hFri
    exact not_party_James (hFriToParty hFri)
  have hDisj : Friday ∨ ¬Watch James := R3 James student_James
  have hNotWatch : ¬Watch James := by
    cases hDisj with
    | inl hFri => exact False.elim (hNotFriday hFri)
    | inr hNW  => exact hNW
  have hStudy : Study James := R5 James hPlan
  have hFinish : Finish James := R4 James student_James hStudy
  have hWatch : Watch James := R2 James hFinish
  exact hNotWatch hWatch
</lean>

ANSWER: No

<lean>
axiom Marketing : Prop          -- they focused budget on marketing (last quarter)
axiom CostsUp : Prop            -- production costs increased (last quarter)
axiom CustomersUp : Prop        -- they gained more customers (last quarter)
axiom SalesUp : Prop            -- sales increased (last quarter)

-- If they invested in marketing, they would gain more customers.
axiom M_to_C : Marketing → CustomersUp
-- With more customers, sales numbers would go up.
axiom C_to_S : CustomersUp → SalesUp
-- Either they focused budget on marketing, or production costs increased, or both.
axiom disj : Marketing ∨ (CostsUp ∨ (Marketing ∧ CostsUp))

-- From the first two premises, marketing implies sales up.
theorem M_to_S : Marketing → SalesUp := by
  intro hm
  apply C_to_S
  exact M_to_C hm

-- Contraposition: if sales did not increase, then they did not focus on marketing.
theorem notS_to_notM : ¬SalesUp → ¬Marketing := by
  intro hns
  intro hm
  apply hns
  exact M_to_S hm

-- From the trilemma, we can derive Marketing ∨ CostsUp.
theorem M_or_C : Marketing ∨ CostsUp := by
  cases disj with
  | inl hm =>
      exact Or.inl hm
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hc =>
          exact Or.inr hc
      | inr hboth =>
          exact Or.inl hboth.left

-- Main derivation: if sales did not increase, then production costs increased.
theorem costs_increased_if_no_sales : ¬SalesUp → CostsUp := by
  intro hns
  have hMorC := M_or_C
  cases hMorC with
  | inl hm =>
      -- This branch leads to a contradiction with ¬SalesUp.
      have hs : SalesUp := M_to_S hm
      exact False.elim (hns hs)
  | inr hc =>
      exact hc
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom James : Person

axiom PracticedHard : Person → Prop
axiom PlayWell : Person → Prop
axiom PickedStart : Person → Prop
axiom Talented : Person → Prop
axiom NaturalAbility : Person → Prop
axiom TopYouth : Person → Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : ∀ x : Person, PracticedHard x → PlayWell x
axiom P2 : ∀ x : Person, PickedStart x → Talented x
axiom P3 : ∀ x : Person, PracticedHard x ∨ ¬Talented x
axiom P4 : ∀ x : Person, NaturalAbility x → PickedStart x
axiom P5 : ∀ x : Person, TopYouth x → NaturalAbility x

-- From "not play well" we can derive "not top youth"
theorem not_playwell_implies_not_top (j : Person) : ¬PlayWell j → ¬TopYouth j := by
  intro hNotPW
  intro hTop
  have hNat : NaturalAbility j := P5 j hTop
  have hPick : PickedStart j := P4 j hNat
  have hTal : Talented j := P2 j hPick
  have hNotPrac : ¬PracticedHard j := by
    intro hPrac
    exact hNotPW (P1 j hPrac)
  have hNotTal : ¬Talented j := by
    have disj := P3 j
    cases disj with
    | inl hPrac =>
        have f : False := hNotPrac hPrac
        exact False.elim f
    | inr hNt => exact hNt
  exact hNotTal hTal

-- This shows that if James did not play well, then the queried implication is false.
theorem conditional_refutation :
  ¬PlayWell James → ¬(¬PlayWell James → TopYouth James) := by
  intro hNotPW
  intro hImp
  have hTop : TopYouth James := hImp hNotPW

axiom A1 : BelowFreezing → SnowOnGround

-- If someone builds a snowman, then there is enough snow.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, BuildsSnowman x → EnoughSnow

-- Either it is below freezing, or there is not enough snow, or both.
axiom A3 : BelowFreezing ∨ ¬EnoughSnow ∨ (BelowFreezing ∧ ¬EnoughSnow)

-- If someone loves winter, then they build snowmen.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, LovesWinter x → BuildsSnowman x

theorem no_snow_implies_mary_not_love : ¬SnowOnGround → ¬LovesWinter Mary := by
  intro hNoSnow
  -- From BelowFreezing → SnowOnGround and ¬SnowOnGround, get ¬BelowFreezing
  have hNotBF : ¬BelowFreezing := fun bf => hNoSnow (A1 bf)
  -- From the trilemma and ¬BelowFreezing, deduce ¬EnoughSnow
  have hNotEnough : ¬EnoughSnow := by
    cases A3 with
    | inl bf =>
      have f : False := hNoSnow (A1 bf)
      exact (fun _ => False.elim f)
    | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hne => exact hne
      | inr bf_and_hne => exact bf_and_hne.right
  -- Now show Mary does not love winter
  intro hLove
  have hBuild : BuildsSnowman Mary := A4 Mary hLove
  have hEnough : EnoughSnow := A2 Mary hBuild
  exact hNotEnough hEnough

axiom R2 : PrepareWell → Ready
-- If someone is diligent, they prepare well for presentations.
axiom R3 : Diligent → PrepareWell
-- Either it is close to lunchtime, or they are not ready, or both.
axiom Cert : CloseLunch ∨ (¬Ready ∨ (CloseLunch ∧ ¬Ready))

-- From not hungry, derive not ready.
theorem notHungry_implies_notReady : ¬Hungry → ¬Ready := by
  intro nh
  have ncl : ¬CloseLunch := by
    intro cl
    exact nh (R1 cl)
  cases Cert with
  | inl cl =>
      exact fun r => False.elim (ncl cl)
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl nr => exact nr
      | inr clnr =>
          exact fun r => False.elim (ncl clnr.left)

-- Diligent implies ready.
theorem diligent_implies_ready : Diligent → Ready := by
  intro d
  exact R2 (R3 d)

-- From not hungry, derive not diligent.
theorem notHungry_implies_notDiligent : ¬Hungry → ¬Diligent := by
  intro nh
  intro d
  have rdy : Ready := diligent_implies_ready d
  have nr : ¬Ready := notHungry_implies_notReady nh
  exact nr rdy

-- Hence, assuming coworkers are not hungry, the implication (¬Hungry → Diligent) is false.
theorem negate_claim_from_nh : (¬Hungry) → ¬(¬Hungry → Diligent) := by
  intro nh
  intro himp
  have nd : ¬Diligent := notHungry_implies_notDiligent nh
  exact nd (himp nh)
</lean>

  intro hNoQuiz

    intro hMon
    exact hNoQuiz (MtoQ hMon)
  -- From the disjunction and ¬Monday, deduce ¬Studied Alex

  intro hGood

axiom OnTimePackage : Prop

axiom CanStart : obj → Prop

axiom CanPickUp : obj → Prop
axiom LearnsSkills : obj → Prop

-- If the package arrives on time, people will be able to start a new project.
axiom A1 : OnTimePackage → ∀ x : obj, CanStart x
-- If the special order is in stock, they can pick up the materials they need.
axiom A2 : SpecialOrderReady → ∀ x : obj, CanPickUp x
-- Either the package will arrive on time or the special order will be ready.
axiom A3 : OnTimePackage ∨ SpecialOrderReady
-- If someone starts his new project, they will learn some new skills.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Starts x → LearnsSkills x

-- Max was unable to pick up the materials he needed.
axiom Max_cannot_pickup : ¬ CanPickUp Max

-- From Max's inability to pick up materials, the special order is not ready.
theorem not_special_order_ready : ¬ SpecialOrderReady :=
by
  intro hSR
  have hPickup : CanPickUp Max := (A2 hSR) Max
  exact Max_cannot_pickup hPickup

-- Therefore, the package arrived on time.
theorem on_time_package : OnTimePackage :=
by
  cases A3 with
  | inl hP => exact hP
  | inr hSR => exact False.elim (not_special_order_ready hSR)

-- Hence, Max can start a new project (ability), but this does not imply he actually starts.
theorem Max_can_start : CanStart Max := (A1 on_time_package) Max
</lean>

    -- From P4 and P2, Payday → PlanBuy Mary
    have hPay_implies_Plan : Payday → PlanBuy Mary := fun hPay => P2 Mary (P4 hPay)

            exact this.elim

  have hNotLate : ¬Late := by

  have hNotUp : ¬UpLate := by

    have hM : MissBus := A4 hU
    exact hNotMiss hM
  -- From BedLate → UpLate and ¬UpLate, deduce ¬BedLate
  intro hB
  have hU : UpLate := A5 hB
  exact hNotUp hU

axiom Alarm : Prop
axiom Ready : Prop
axiom MissBus : Prop
axiom Late : Prop
axiom UpLate : Prop
axiom BedLate : Prop

-- If the alarm went off, then it was time to get ready.
axiom A1 : Alarm → Ready
-- If they missed the bus, then they'd be late for school.
axiom A2 : MissBus → Late
-- Either the alarm went off, or they weren't late for school.
axiom A3 : Alarm ∨ ¬Late
-- If they got up late, then they miss the bus.
axiom A4 : UpLate → MissBus
-- If they went to bed late, then they get up late.
axiom A5 : BedLate → UpLate
-- John knows it is not the time to get ready.
axiom J1 : ¬Ready

theorem john_not_bedlate : ¬BedLate := by
  -- From Alarm → Ready and ¬Ready, deduce ¬Alarm
  have hNotAlarm : ¬Alarm := by
    intro hA
    have hR : Ready := A1 hA
    exact J1 hR
  -- From Alarm ∨ ¬Late and ¬Alarm, deduce ¬Late
  have hNotLate : ¬Late := by
    apply Or.elim A3
    · intro hA
      intro _; exact (hNotAlarm hA)
    · intro hNL
      exact hNL
  -- From MissBus → Late and ¬Late, deduce ¬MissBus
  have hNotMiss : ¬MissBus := by
    intro hM
    have hL : Late := A2 hM
    exact hNotLate hL
  -- From UpLate → MissBus and ¬MissBus, deduce ¬UpLate
  have hNotUp : ¬UpLate := by
    intro hU
    have hM : MissBus := A4 hU
    exact hNotMiss hM
  -- From BedLate → UpLate and ¬UpLate, deduce ¬BedLate
  intro hB
  have hU : UpLate := A5 hB
  exact hNotUp hU

axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, Stirs x → Thick

axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, Tastes x → Stirs x
axiom Secret : ∃ x : Person, (Tastes x ∨ Salad x ∨ (Tastes x ∧ Salad x))

theorem no_salt_of_not_savory (hns : ¬ Savory) : ¬ (∃ x : Person, AddsSalt x) := by
  intro hex
  apply Exists.elim hex
  intro x hx
  exact hns (R1 x hx)

  have hnsalt : ¬ (∃ x : Person, AddsSalt x) := no_salt_of_not_savory hns
  have disj := Law1
  cases disj with
  | inl hexsalt =>
      exact False.elim (hnsalt hexsalt)
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hnt => exact hnt
      | inr hboth =>
          have hex := hboth.left
          exact False.elim (hnsalt hex)

  intro hex
  apply Exists.elim hex
  intro x hx
  exact hnt (R2 x hx)

theorem no_taste_of_not_thick (hnt : ¬ Thick) : ¬ (∃ x : Person, Tastes x) := by
  intro hex
  apply Exists.elim hex
  intro x hx
  have hxstir : Stirs x := R3 x hx
  exact hnt (R2 x hxstir)

theorem salad_exists_of_not_savory (hns : ¬ Savory) : ∃ x : Person, Salad x := by
  have hnt : ¬ Thick := not_thick_of_not_savory hns
  have hnotaste : ¬ (∃ x : Person, Tastes x) := no_taste_of_not_thick hnt
  apply Exists.elim Secret
  intro x hx
  cases hx with
  | inl htaste =>
      have : False := hnotaste (Exists.intro x htaste)
      exact this.elim
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hsal => exact Exists.intro x hsal
      | inr hboth => exact Exists.intro x hboth.right
</lean>

axiom Person : Type
axiom James : Person

axiom ForecastRain : Prop
axiom GoOut : Person → Prop
axiom BringUmbrella : Person → Prop

axiom BigGame : Prop
axiom FriendsGetTogether : Prop

axiom HighViewership : Prop
axiom GoPlayOut : Prop

-- Premises from the context
-- 1) If forecast predicts rain, then people will bring an umbrella when they go out.
axiom H1 : ForecastRain → ∀ p : Person, GoOut p → BringUmbrella p
-- 2) If there is a big game on TV, then friends will get together to watch it.
axiom H2 : BigGame → FriendsGetTogether
-- 3) Either rain is forecasted, or friends did not get together to watch the game, or rain is forecasted and friends did not get together to watch.
axiom H3 : ForecastRain ∨ ¬FriendsGetTogether ∨ (ForecastRain ∧ ¬FriendsGetTogether)
-- 4) If the TV viewership is high, then there is a big game on TV.
axiom H4 : HighViewership → BigGame
-- 5) Either the TV viewership is high, or they all decide to go play out, or both.
axiom H5 : HighViewership ∨ GoPlayOut ∨ (HighViewership ∧ GoPlayOut)

-- If they all decide to go play out, James goes out (he is among "they all").
axiom H6 : GoPlayOut → GoOut James

-- Assumption to evaluate with: James did not bring an umbrella.
axiom NotUmbJames : ¬BringUmbrella James

-- Useful consequences

-- From "James did not bring an umbrella" and the rain rule, James cannot both have rain forecast and go out.
theorem no_rain_and_out : ¬(ForecastRain ∧ GoOut James) := by
  intro h
  have hr : ForecastRain := And.left h
  have hj : GoOut James := And.right h
  have bj : BringUmbrella James := (H1 hr James) hj
  exact NotUmbJames bj

-- If friends got together, then rain must have been forecasted (from H3).
theorem rain_if_friends : FriendsGetTogether → ForecastRain := by
  intro f
  cases H3 with
  | inl fr =>
    exact fr
  | inr h =>
    cases h with
    | inl nfg =>
      have : False := nfg f
      exact False.elim this
    | inr fr_and_nfg =>
      exact And.left fr_and_nfg

-- If viewership is high, then rain was forecasted.
theorem rain_if_highview : HighViewership → ForecastRain := by
  intro hv
  have bg : BigGame := H4 hv
  have fg : FriendsGetTogether := H2 bg
  exact rain_if_friends fg

-- It is impossible that both high viewership and "they all go play out" hold together under NotUmbJames.
theorem hv_and_goplayout_impossible : HighViewership ∧ GoPlayOut → False := by
  intro h
  have hv : HighViewership := And.left h
  have gpo : GoPlayOut := And.right h
  have fr : ForecastRain := rain_if_highview hv
  have jo : GoOut James := H6 gpo
  apply no_rain_and_out
  exact And.intro fr jo
</lean>

axiom KitchenHot : Prop
axiom StoveOn : Prop

axiom Hungry : obj → Prop
axiom AteDinner : obj → Prop

-- If the stove is on, the kitchen gets hot.
axiom A1 : StoveOn → KitchenHot
-- If someone makes pancakes, they use the stove.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, MakePancakes x → UseStove x
-- Either the stove was on, or they did not use the stove (for any person).
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, StoveOn ∨ ¬UseStove x
-- If someone is hungry, they make pancakes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Hungry x → MakePancakes x
-- If Sam did not eat dinner last night, he was hungry this morning.
axiom A5 : ¬AteDinner Sam → Hungry Sam

theorem use_stove_implies_on (x : obj) (hx : UseStove x) : StoveOn := by
  have h := A3 x
  apply Or.elim h
  · intro hOn
    exact hOn
  · intro hNotUse
    exact False.elim (hNotUse hx)

theorem not_dinner_implies_hot : ¬AteDinner Sam → KitchenHot := by
  intro hNoDinner
  have hHun : Hungry Sam := A5 hNoDinner
  have hPanc : MakePancakes Sam := A4 Sam hHun
  have hUse : UseStove Sam := A2 Sam hPanc
  have hOn : StoveOn := use_stove_implies_on Sam hUse
  exact A1 hOn

-- From "the kitchen did not get hot", we can derive "Sam ate dinner last night".
theorem not_hot_implies_dinner : ¬KitchenHot → AteDinner Sam := by
  intro hNotHot
  by_contra hNoDinner
  exact hNotHot (not_dinner_implies_hot hNoDinner)
</lean>
